explanations.2: This sets a default PHP version that can be overridden when building the Docker image, like docker build --build-arg PHP_VERSION=8.3.
explanations.7: This is a build argument that defaults to 'true', controlling whether php-cs-fixer (a code formatting tool) gets installed in the Docker image.
explanations.9: This specifies which version of php-cs-fixer to download; the version number can be overridden at build time.
explanations.14: The ${PHP_VERSION} syntax pulls in the value from line 2, so the Docker image will be built on the PHP version you specified earlier.
explanations.19: This re-declares the PHPCSFIXER_INSTALL argument inside the Docker build stage so it can be used in the RUN command that follows.
explanations.24: The 'set -e -x' makes the script stop on errors and print each command, helping you see what's happening during the Docker build.
explanations.26: This checks if PHPCSFIXER_INSTALL equals the string 'true', and only runs the installation commands if that condition is met.
explanations.28: This prints a colorful message to the Docker build output; the \e codes are escape sequences that make the text appear in a bright magenta background.
explanations.30: This downloads the php-cs-fixer tool from GitHub using curl and saves it to /usr/local/bin where executable programs are typically stored.
explanations.32: The chmod +x command makes the downloaded file executable, like giving it permission to run as a program.
